_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about what makes people similar to each other and what makes us different — things like our age, what we can do, the languages we speak, our culture and our beliefs.

_source.w: We are learning about what makes people similar to each other and what makes us different — things like our age, what we can do, the languages we speak, our culture and our beliefs.
y: When we notice both similarities and differences, we realise that every person brings something special to our classroom and community.
Understanding differences helps us be kind and welcoming to people whose lives look different from ours.
Learning about diversity now helps us build friendships with all kinds of people throughout our lives.
eg: We survey our class to find out how many different languages are spoken at home and make a colourful chart showing all of them.
We describe a friend and find one thing we have in common and one thing that is different between us.
We read a story about children from different cultural backgrounds and talk about what they share and what is unique to each.
We draw self-portraits showing the things that make us who we are — our family, hobbies, foods we love and traditions we celebrate.
We play a game where we stand up if something is true for us, and see how some things are shared by lots of us while others are unique.
We talk about how people in our family have different abilities and ages, and how everyone still belongs and matters.
